steps.title: Test the alarm-call link
steps.content: Navarro and colleagues concluded that H. ochraceiceps associates T. caesius alarm calls with danger. The strongest support would show that the birds respond to the calls specifically, not just to any sound.

steps.title: Use the control sound
steps.content: The matching finding supplies that control: random noise produced no reaction, so the response was tied to the alarm calls. Choice B concerns other species, Choice C shows consistency across callers without ruling out noise itself, and Choice D introduces a competing explanation involving the researchers' approach. The answer is A.
